opportunities.title: FEMA — Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities (BRIC) FY 2024–2025
opportunities.one_liner: A $1 billion federal competitive grant program funding shovel-ready infrastructure projects that reduce flood and hazard risk in communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: FEMA has published the FY 2024 and 2025 BRIC funding opportunity, making $1 billion in federal funding available to states, local governments, territories, and Tribal Nations to take proactive steps to protect communities from disasters including floods. BRIC provides funding for public infrastructure projects and mitigation efforts that bolster a community's flood resilience before a disaster strikes, with a special emphasis on shovel-ready construction projects or adoption of hazard-resistant building codes. Federal funding covers up to 75 percent of eligible activity cost, and small, impoverished communities may be eligible for up to a 90 percent federal cost share. Applicants and subapplicants must have a FEMA-approved hazard mitigation plan by the application deadline.
opportunities.eligibility: States, local governments, territories, Tribal Nations (via FEMA GO portal)
opportunities.budget: $1 billion total; max $20M federal share per project (National Competition)

opportunities.title: FEMA — Flood Mitigation Assistance (FMA) FY 2024
opportunities.one_liner: A $600 million competitive grant specifically targeting flood risk reduction for NFIP-insured properties through acquisition, elevation, and flood-proofing projects.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: For fiscal year 2024, up to $600 million is available to states, local communities, Tribal Nations, and territories to reduce or eliminate the risk of repetitive flood damage to buildings insured under the National Flood Insurance Program. FEMA published an amendment to the funding opportunity on April 30, 2026, incorporating recent federal guidance; funding comes from the National Flood Insurance Fund and the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. The goal of the FMA grant program is to employ cost-effective measures to reduce or eliminate the long-term risk of flood damage to buildings, manufactured homes, and other structures insured under the NFIP. Applicants have until 3 p.m. ET on August 6, 2026, to submit or revise and re-submit their application to FEMA.
opportunities.eligibility: States, territories, local governments, federally recognized Tribal Nations

opportunities.title: NFWF — National Coastal Resilience Fund (NCRF) 2026
opportunities.one_liner: A NOAA-backed foundation grant funding nature-based solutions to protect coastal communities from storms, flooding, and erosion, with awards up to $7 million.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The National Coastal Resilience Fund (NCRF), established in 2018, invests in nature-based solutions that protect coastal communities while enhancing habitats for fish and wildlife, including coastal marshes, wetlands, dune and beach systems, oyster and coral reefs, rivers, and floodplains. NFWF has closed pre-proposals for its 2026 NCRF and is moving selected applicants into the full proposal phase; full proposals are due June 24, 2026, with awards announced November–December 2026. Implementation awards typically range from $1 million to $7 million, while planning and design awards range from $100,000 to $1.5 million. Eligible applicants include 501(c) nonprofits, state and local governments, tribal governments and organizations, educational institutions, and commercial entities in US coastal and Great Lakes states and territories.
opportunities.eligibility: Nonprofits, state/local/tribal governments, universities, commercial entities; US coastal/Great Lakes states and territories; invite-only after pre-proposal stage

opportunities.title: EPA — Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants (OSG)
opportunities.one_liner: Rolling federal grants to states for green and gray stormwater infrastructure projects that reduce flooding and improve water quality in communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The EPA's Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants Program helps communities and states reduce water pollution caused by stormwater and sewer runoff, funding green and gray infrastructure projects that strengthen stormwater collection systems, improve flood resilience, and address water quality issues. EPA announced $52 million in grants to help communities improve essential stormwater infrastructure, implementing new requirements under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to help states support projects in small and financially distressed communities. The OSG program is administered by states and deadlines may differ; applicants should approach their state Clean Water State Revolving Fund Program or EPA Regional Office for specific deadlines.
opportunities.eligibility: Municipalities and local governments (via state programs)

opportunities.title: NSF — Confronting Hazards, Impacts and Risks for a Resilient Planet (CHIRRP)
opportunities.one_liner: An NSF standing research program funding transformative Earth system science on hazard knowledge and risk mitigation, including flood and coastal hazards, in partnership with communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The CHIRRP program invites projects focusing on innovative and transformative research that advances Earth system hazard knowledge and risk mitigation in partnership with affected communities, addressing hazards compounded by changing climates, rising populations, and aging infrastructure. Resilience to natural hazards remains a high priority for NSF, which strongly encourages researchers to submit proposals for fundamental research that could yield insights useful to advance natural disaster resilience. NSF supports research into natural hazards such as hurricanes, floods, wildfires, coastal erosion, and severe thunderstorms, and methods to reduce their harmful impacts. This is a standing program accepting proposals on an ongoing basis through NSF's standard proposal submission process.
opportunities.eligibility: US universities, research institutions, community organizations
opportunities.budget: Varies per proposal (standard NSF research grants)

opportunities.title: UKRI / EPSRC — NetworkPlus in Property Flood Resilience
opportunities.funder: UKRI / Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC)
opportunities.one_liner: A UK Research and Innovation award of up to £2.5 million to establish a research network advancing science and practice for property-level flood resilience.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: EPSRC will fund 80% of the full economic cost of up to £2.5 million for a duration of up to three years to establish a NetworkPlus focused on property-level flood resilience. The funding opportunity is invite-only; potential applicants were expected to attend a consortium building workshop on March 23, 2026, in Swindon to be invited to apply. The program is specifically focused on building a UK research community around property-level flood resilience measures, bridging gaps between research, policy, and practice. The funding opportunity was set to open in spring 2026, with more information available on the UKRI page.
opportunities.eligibility: UK research organisations (consortium required; invite-only after workshop attendance)
opportunities.budget: Up to £2.5 million (80% funded by EPSRC)

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-06: Improving Climate Resilience of Navigable Inland Waterways and Related Water Infrastructure
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe (EU Mission: Adaptation to Climate Change)
opportunities.one_liner: A €6 million Horizon Europe Mission grant to improve the flood and climate resilience of inland waterways through systemic nature-based and engineering solutions.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: This Horizon Europe funding call focuses on improving climate resilience of navigable inland waterways and related water infrastructure, where climate and environmental risks such as flooding and ecological degradation are addressed in a systemic way. Cost-efficient and environmentally friendly measures—especially nature-based solutions—are identified with stakeholder involvement to improve climate resilience while supporting integrated co-benefits. Applications are open to consortia with at least 3 participants from different countries based in EU Member States, Associated Countries, and low- and middle-income countries. This Research and Innovation Action provides up to €6 million for up to 5 years, with applications closing on September 23, 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia of ≥3 organisations from EU Member States/Associated Countries plus low- and middle-income countries

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — Disaster Resilient Society for Europe (Cluster 3) 2026
opportunities.one_liner: A Horizon Europe Cluster 3 call targeting innovation for disaster-resilient European societies, including tools for flood risk awareness and disaster response, with proposals due November 2026.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Horizon Europe 2026 Disaster Resilient Society for Europe call has proposals due by November 5, 2026, covering five topics including innovative tools for search and rescue in hazardous conditions and driving innovation uptake of disaster risk solutions. One topic, HORIZON-CL3-2026-01-DRS-05, focuses on climate security and civil preparedness—developing new ways to create pre- and post-crisis climate-change-related scenarios for a more resilient Europe. This cluster funds applied research and innovation actions linking technology developers, civil protection agencies, and local authorities to develop and deploy flood and disaster resilience solutions at scale across Europe. Funding per project typically ranges from €4–10 million.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia from EU Member States and Associated Countries

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe PCP WISE — Water Intelligence Products and Services Powered by Earth Observation
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe (PCP WISE consortium of 12 public buyers)
opportunities.one_liner: A pre-commercial procurement call co-funded by Horizon Europe to develop next-generation EO-powered flood monitoring and stormwater management tools.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Launched on September 5, 2025, the PCP WISE group of 12 buyers issued a Call for Tenders to co-develop next-generation water intelligence products and services powered by Earth Observation, funded under Horizon Europe's Pre-Commercial Procurement mechanism. This three-phase PCP funds R&D services—from solution design to prototype development and field validation—targeting TRL 8 solutions that monitor the Soil-Water-Vegetation system and help predict, prevent, and manage water-related crises such as floods, fires, and droughts. One focus area is Urban Water Excess (East-North Europe), specifically targeting flood and stormwater management with real-time tools. Eligible participants include technology companies and research organisations from EU and associated countries.
opportunities.eligibility: Technology companies and research organisations from EU and associated countries
opportunities.budget: Multi-phase R&D contract funding (exact envelope per phase to be confirmed)

opportunities.title: EPA Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants (OSG Program)
opportunities.funder: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), administered through states
opportunities.one_liner: EPA program funding green and gray infrastructure to reduce stormwater runoff, sewer overflows, and urban flooding.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The EPA's Sewer Overflow and Stormwater Reuse Municipal Grants Program helps communities and states reduce water pollution caused by stormwater and sewer runoff, funding green and gray infrastructure projects that strengthen stormwater collection systems, improve flood resilience, and address water quality issues. The OSG program is administered by states and deadlines may differ; applicants should approach their state Clean Water State Revolving Fund program or EPA Regional Office for more information on specific deadlines. This program is particularly relevant for urban and pluvial flood risk reduction.
opportunities.eligibility: Municipalities and states

opportunities.title: EPA Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) Program
opportunities.one_liner: EPA's flexible, low-cost federal loan program for significant water and stormwater infrastructure projects that enhance community flood resilience.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) accelerates investment in water infrastructure by offering flexible financing for significant drinking water and wastewater infrastructure projects, providing long-term loans to help communities implement critical water infrastructure at lower cost. EPA's WIFIA program has announced $6.5 billion in WIFIA funding available for water systems, with an additional $550 million available to states through the State WIFIA (SWIFIA) program. Letters of Interest are accepted on a continuous, rolling basis. Projects eligible include stormwater runoff mitigation and flood-resilient water infrastructure.
opportunities.eligibility: Water utilities, municipalities, states, and other eligible water infrastructure project sponsors
opportunities.budget: $6.5 billion in WIFIA funding; additional $550 million via State WIFIA (SWIFIA)

opportunities.title: FEMA FY2026 Pre-Disaster Mitigation (PDM) Grant Program
opportunities.one_liner: FY2026 congressionally directed pre-disaster mitigation grants primarily focused on infrastructure improvements to reduce flooding risk.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The FEMA Pre-Disaster Mitigation Grant Program funds Congressionally Directed Spending projects for state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, designed to implement cost-effective measures that reduce natural hazard risk to people and property and decrease reliance on federal funding for future disasters. In FY2026, the PDM Grant Program will award $189,713,659 to projects identified in the 2026 Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act's Joint Explanatory Statement. The FY2026 PDM projects come from 40 states and one tribe, with most projects focused on improving infrastructure to reduce flooding risk.
opportunities.eligibility: State, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments identified in the FY2026 DHS Appropriations Act

opportunities.title: EU LIFE Programme Climate Change Adaptation Strand (2026 Call)
opportunities.one_liner: EU LIFE programme funding implementation-focused climate adaptation projects including nature-based flood risk solutions.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The LIFE Climate Change Adaptation strand funds projects that strengthen resilience, reduce climate vulnerability, and support implementation of EU adaptation policies, with an indicative €28 million budget for 2026 that prioritises risk assessment tools, nature-based solutions, climate-proof infrastructure, and systemic policy integration. The strand supports practical, scalable solutions that improve resilience across sectors, ecosystems, and governance levels while contributing to a climate-resilient Europe by 2050. Projects must demonstrate measurable impact, avoid maladaptation, and align with the European Climate Law and EU Adaptation Strategy.
opportunities.eligibility: Public and private organisations from EU member states and associated countries
opportunities.budget: ~€28 million indicative budget for 2026 adaptation strand

opportunities.title: Florida DEP — Resilient Florida Program (2026 Application Cycle)
opportunities.one_liner: Florida's flagship coastal and flood resilience grant program opens for its 2026 cycle in July, funding both planning and implementation projects addressing flooding and sea level rise.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Resilient Florida Program application portal will be open for formal submittal from July 1 to September 1, 2026, for planning grants and the Fiscal Year 2027–28 Statewide Flooding and Sea Level Rise Resilience Plan. Eligible applicants include counties, municipalities, special districts with specific responsibilities, and regional resilience entities, who may receive funding to analyze vulnerabilities as well as implement adaptation and mitigation projects. The program is hosting virtual office hours from April through June 2026 to assist prospective applicants with eligibility checks, draft application review, and GIS assistance. The 2026 portal opened for drafting on April 1, 2026, giving applicants lead time to prepare full submissions before the formal window opens.
opportunities.eligibility: Florida counties, municipalities, special districts with specific responsibilities, and regional resilience entities

opportunities.title: New York State DEC — Resilient NY Watershed Grant (RWG)
opportunities.funder: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC)
opportunities.one_liner: New York's Resilient NY Watershed Grant offers a share of $100 million to communities for climate resiliency and flood-risk reduction projects, with a June 26 deadline.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Unprecedented storms are becoming more common each year, resulting in significant flooding in communities across New York State; in response, the NY Department of Environmental Conservation established the Resilient NY program in November 2018. A combined $100 million for climate resiliency projects is available through RWG and NYS Department of State programs. The CFA application deadline is Friday, June 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m.; a webinar was held in April 2026 covering funding opportunities through RWG and NYSDOS' Community Resilience and Flood Risk Reduction Grants. Watersheds with significant flooding and related hazards to public safety can be enrolled in the Resilient NY program to receive a Flood Mitigation & Resilience Report.
opportunities.eligibility: Local governments, municipalities, and watershed organizations in New York State
opportunities.budget: $100 million combined across RWG and NYS DOS programs

opportunities.title: UK Environment Agency — Flood & Coastal Erosion Risk Management (FCERM) Grant-in-Aid Investment Programme
opportunities.one_liner: England's new 10-year FCERM investment programme just opened, with at least £300 million earmarked for natural flood management and project submissions accepted from July 2026.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The next FCERM investment programme opened on April 1, 2026, with the Environment Agency hosting webinars starting in early May to explain the process and expecting project submissions from July 2026 onward. There is a strong focus on natural flood management (NFM) as a way of reducing flood risk while delivering multiple benefits; a minimum of 3% of FCERM investment will go towards NFM over the first three years and a minimum of 4% over 10 years, amounting to at least £300 million in NFM funding. The guidance now also includes non-Risk Management Authorities, meaning NGOs, landowners, and other non-traditional bodies can apply for the first time. Standalone NFM projects may include river and floodplain management, woodland management, run-off management, and coast and estuary management.
opportunities.eligibility: Risk Management Authorities (local authorities, water companies, internal drainage boards) and, for NFM projects, non-RMAs including NGOs and landowners in England
opportunities.budget: Minimum £300 million ring-fenced for natural flood management over 10 years; programme is multi-billion overall

opportunities.title: EU LIFE Programme — Climate Change Adaptation (LIFE-2025-SAP-CLIMA-CCA)
opportunities.one_liner: The EU LIFE programme's climate adaptation strand funds nature-based solutions for flood mitigation in rural, urban, and coastal settings across Europe.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Projects supported under this call should foster the use of nature-based solutions to improve climate resilience, particularly in coastal zones, wetlands, estuaries, and riparian areas; actions may include restoring degraded habitats, using natural infrastructure to manage climate risks like flooding or sea-level rise, and promoting biodiversity-based adaptation. The call also covers water management projects that protect and restore wetlands, ensure a stable freshwater supply, and implement measures to reduce water use while maintaining water quality. The LIFE programme has a long track record of funding practical, on-the-ground adaptation projects across EU member states, and this strand is specifically designed to move nature-based flood solutions from pilot to scale. Typical LIFE grants range from approximately €1 million to €10 million per project.
opportunities.eligibility: Public bodies, NGOs, and private companies; typically 2 or more partners including at least one EU entity

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — Innovative Solutions for the European Water Resilience Strategy (HORIZON-CL6-2027-02-CLIMATE-01)
opportunities.one_liner: A forward-looking Horizon Europe two-stage call seeking innovations in flood protection, water cycle restoration, and drought/flood adaptation for implementation at real-world scale.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: This EU funding call covers innovative solutions to implement the European Water Resilience Strategy, including development, testing, and validation of technologies and approaches for the restoration of the water cycle, water efficiency, drought adaptation, flood protection, and ensuring clean and affordable water supply. Successful applications require the involvement of at least three independent partners from different countries, the application of the multi-actor approach, and compliance with Horizon Europe financial and operational eligibility criteria. The call supports consortia implementing novel or disruptive approaches such as source-to-sea methods, digital monitoring systems, circular water use, and participatory water management, engaging municipal administrations, water authorities, farmers, and NGOs. Deadlines are Stage 1 on April 8, 2027, and Stage 2 on September 14, 2027, providing ample lead time for consortium formation.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia of at least 3 independent partners from different countries; eligible entities include universities, industry, municipal authorities, water utilities, and NGOs

opportunities.title: ADB — Climate Change Fund (CCF): Adaptation Window
opportunities.one_liner: ADB's Climate Change Fund provides rolling grant financing for climate adaptation projects — including flood resilience — across all developing member countries in Asia and the Pacific.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Climate Change Fund (CCF) was established in May 2008 to facilitate greater investments in developing member countries to address the causes and consequences of climate change, playing a key role in mainstreaming climate actions in ADB operations. The fund provides financing through grant components of investments, technical assistance (stand-alone and linked to loans), and direct charge; it has three components: adaptation, clean energy development, and REDD+ and land use. Applications are reviewed in six batches per year, due on January 31, March 31, May 31, July 31, September 30, and November 30, with direct charge applications reviewed on an ongoing basis. ADB promotes disaster risk reduction and climate change adaptation by investing in flood control, water supply, transport, and energy infrastructure that withstands disasters and climate change.
opportunities.eligibility: ADB Developing Member Countries (DMCs); proposals must be submitted through ADB project teams
opportunities.budget: Total fund approximately $98 million (ADB net income); individual grant amounts vary

opportunities.title: Green Climate Fund (GCF) — Adaptation Portfolio (Rolling / Readiness Programme)
opportunities.one_liner: The GCF's adaptation portfolio funds flood and coastal resilience projects in developing nations on a rolling basis, with a streamlined pathway for projects under $10 million.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The GCF's Readiness Programme and Project Preparation Facility are available on a rolling basis to support development of adaptation project concept notes, feasibility studies, and full project proposals. The Simplified Approval Process (SAP) provides a route for smaller adaptation project proposals up to USD 10 million to be approved more quickly. Adaptation needs are coming into sharp focus as increasing numbers of people worldwide are affected by higher frequency and severity of water-related disasters, such as droughts, floods, tropical cyclones, and storm surges. The Fund aims for a 50:50 balance between mitigation and adaptation investments over time, with a floor of 50% of the adaptation allocation for particularly vulnerable countries including Least Developed Countries, Small Island Developing States, and African States.
opportunities.eligibility: National Designated Authorities (NDAs) and Accredited Entities in GCF-eligible developing countries
opportunities.budget: Up to $10 million via Simplified Approval Process; larger projects have no stated cap

opportunities.title: ADB — Integrated Flood Resilience and Adaptation Project (Philippines, Phase 1)
opportunities.one_liner: ADB's active flood resilience project in the Philippines offers procurement and technical assistance opportunities for firms and agencies working on riverine flood infrastructure and community-based flood risk management.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Integrated Flood Resilience and Adaptation Project Phase 1 will reduce climate risks and enable adaptation in three major river basins in the Philippines — Abra, Ranao/Agus, and Tagum-Libuganon — by improving strategic flood risk management planning, constructing flood protection infrastructure, and strengthening community-based flood risk management. The Philippine Development Plan 2023–2028 calls for upgrading and expanding flood control infrastructure combined with nature-based solutions, enhancing institutional capacities and decision support systems, and prioritizing support to highly vulnerable local government units. The project river basins are subject to high climate hazards including rising sea levels, increasing precipitation intensity, and more severe onshore storms, which combined will further increase flood risks. Firms and researchers can engage through ADB's Business Opportunities portal for procurement notices linked to this project.
opportunities.eligibility: Philippine national government agencies, local governments, and contractors/consultants responding to ADB procurement notices

opportunities.title: Canada — Build Communities Strong Fund (Infrastructure Canada) — Climate Adaptation Stream
opportunities.one_liner: Canada's new Build Communities Strong Fund — the successor to DMAF and ICIP — launches in 2026–27 with $51 billion over 10 years for infrastructure including climate adaptation and flood resilience projects.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Build Communities Strong Fund will invest in a wide range of infrastructure projects supporting economic prosperity, housing, education, health, transit, and climate adaptation, providing $51 billion over 10 years starting in 2026–27, and $3 billion per year ongoing to support new and renewed public infrastructure. Canada's predecessor program, the Disaster Mitigation and Adaptation Fund (DMAF), is now fully allocated and closed, making this new fund the large-scale successor for flood-related infrastructure investment. Canadian municipalities and provinces working on flood infrastructure should monitor Infrastructure Canada's website closely for the new program's intake guidelines and deadlines. Program details, including specific application windows and eligibility criteria for the climate adaptation stream, are forthcoming.
opportunities.eligibility: Provinces, territories, municipalities, and Indigenous communities in Canada
opportunities.budget: $51 billion over 10 years; $3 billion per year ongoing

opportunities.title: Florida DEP — Resilient Florida Program 2026 Application Cycle
opportunities.one_liner: Florida's primary state grant program funds local vulnerability assessments, resilience planning, and capital infrastructure projects addressing flooding and sea level rise.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Resilient Florida Program includes grants available to counties, municipalities, special districts, and regional resilience entities, with eligible applicants able to receive funding to analyze and plan for vulnerabilities as well as implement projects for adaptation and mitigation. The Program is hosting virtual office hours from April through June 2026 to assist prospective applicants for the 2026 application cycle, with the Planning and Policy Team available for eligibility checks, draft application review, and GIS assistance on Thursdays 9:00–11:00 a.m. EDT through June 25, 2026. The prior cycle's portal was open for submittals from July 7, 2025 to September 1, 2025 for Planning Grants and Implementation Grants to be included in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Statewide Flooding and Sea Level Rise Resilience Plan. The 2026 cycle is expected to follow a similar July–September window.
opportunities.eligibility: Florida counties, municipalities, special districts, regional resilience entities

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — Restore Our Ocean and Waters Mission (2027 Call Opening 2026)
opportunities.one_liner: Upcoming Horizon Europe calls target increasing riparian and coastal-area resilience to climate change, including waterfront cities and islands.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Upcoming calls include HORIZON-MISS-2027-03-OCEAN-01 on 'Increasing riparian and coastal areas resilience to climate change, including in waterfront cities and islands' and HORIZON-MISS-2027-03-OCEAN-05 on large-scale demonstration of protection, restoration, and management of blue forest ecosystems across European sea basins. A total of 30 consortia have already signed grant agreements under the latest Horizon Europe Missions calls, contributing to climate adaptation and the restoration of oceans and waters missions. The Mission engages a wide range of stakeholders, including public and private organisations, national, regional, and local actors, coastal and riparian communities, businesses, and citizens. Partner-brokerage events to build consortia are currently active.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia of research institutions, local/coastal authorities, businesses, NGOs in EU member states and associated countries

opportunities.title: EU LIFE Programme — Calls for Proposals 2026
opportunities.funder: European Climate, Infrastructure and Environment Executive Agency (CINEA)
opportunities.one_liner: The EU LIFE Programme's 2026 calls include dedicated funding tracks for nature-based solutions, climate adaptation, and ecosystem restoration that can address flood mitigation.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The LIFE Programme is the EU's dedicated funding instrument for environment, nature, and climate action. The LIFE 2026 Calls for Proposals are now published in the EU Funding & Tenders Portal, with submission deadlines listed for all calls. LIFE supports projects that implement, demonstrate, and promote innovative techniques and approaches across nature & biodiversity, circular economy, climate change mitigation, and climate change adaptation sub-programmes. Flood-relevant topics typically fall under the Climate Change Adaptation and Nature & Biodiversity sub-programmes, covering nature-based solutions, wetland restoration, and urban stormwater management.
opportunities.eligibility: Public authorities, NGOs, private companies, research bodies in EU member states and some non-EU countries

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — Land, Oceans & Water for Climate Action (Cluster 6, 2026 Call)
opportunities.one_liner: Horizon Europe Cluster 6 funds research on strengthening water resilience to climate and socio-economic impacts, including flood risk in river systems.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Horizon Europe 2025 two-stage call features one topic on 'Strengthening water resilience to climate and socio-economic impacts,' with the second stage open for submission as of February 18, 2026. The Horizon Europe 2026 call for proposals opened February 12, 2026, with topics including effective climate change adaptation in agriculture and forestry, and five topics covering safeguarding global ocean health and strengthening resilience of water systems. All funding information and application procedures are accessible on the EU Funding and Tenders Portal.
opportunities.eligibility: Research consortia, public bodies, technology providers in EU member states and associated countries

opportunities.title: New York State Environmental Bond Act — Community Resilience & Flood Risk Reduction Grant Program
opportunities.funder: New York State Department of State (DOS) / NY Environmental Bond Act
opportunities.one_liner: New York's $4.2B Environmental Bond Act is funding $40 million in flood risk reduction and coastal resilience projects for municipalities across the state, due June 26, 2026.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Governor Hochul announced nearly $100 million in new grant funding to support nature-based and green infrastructure projects designed to reduce flood risk and enhance community resilience to extreme weather. The Community Resilience and Flood Risk Reduction Grant Program makes $40 million available through two categories: $20 million for Coastal Rehabilitation and Resilience and $20 million for Inland Flooding and Local Waterfront Revitalization Program Implementation. Applications can be submitted through the Consolidated Funding Application (CFA) portal and are due by 4 p.m. on Friday, June 26, 2026. Eligible applicants are NY coastal and inland municipalities participating in the Local Waterfront Revitalization Program.
opportunities.eligibility: NY coastal and inland municipalities participating in the Local Waterfront Revitalization Program (LWRP)

opportunities.title: Texas Water Development Board — Flood Infrastructure Fund (FIF) SFY 2026–27
opportunities.one_liner: Texas's Flood Infrastructure Fund provides zero-interest loans and grants to local political subdivisions for flood planning, mitigation, and control projects recommended in the state flood plan.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: Authorized by the Texas Legislature and approved by Texas voters through a constitutional amendment, the FIF program provides zero-interest loans and grants for flood control, flood mitigation, and drainage projects. Only Flood Management Evaluations, Flood Mitigation Projects, and Flood Management Strategies recommended in the approved state flood plan are eligible for financial assistance. The public comment period for the SFY 2026–27 FIF Intended Use Plan is currently open and will close on June 12, 2026; following the close of the public comment period, the TWDB will post the abridged application and begin the solicitation period. Funding can support planning, design, construction, and rehabilitation of flood projects, whether structural or non-structural including nature-based.
opportunities.eligibility: Texas political subdivisions: cities, counties, and designated districts/authorities
opportunities.budget: Mix of zero-interest loans and grants (up to 100% grant for low-income disaster-affected communities)

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change (HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-06): Inland Waterways Climate Resilience
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe — EU Missions
opportunities.one_liner: This Horizon Europe Mission call funds research and innovation consortia improving the climate resilience of inland waterways and related water infrastructure, with emphasis on nature-based solutions.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The funding call addresses climate and environmental risks to water infrastructure including droughts, flooding, slow onset events, ecological degradation, and cascading and compound events in a systemic way. Cost-efficient and environmentally friendly measures — especially nature-based solutions — are identified with stakeholder involvement to improve climate resilience of inland waterways while supporting integrated co-benefits and avoiding competing water uses across countries and regions. Applications are open for consortia with at least 3 participants established in different countries, based in EU Member States and Associated Countries, plus low- and middle-income countries. This is a Research and Innovation Action providing up to €6 million for up to 5 years, with applications closing September 23, 2026.
opportunities.eligibility: Consortia of ≥3 participants from different countries (EU/Associated Countries); minimum one partner from an EU Member State; low- and middle-income countries also eligible

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe — EU Mission on Adaptation (HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-04): Bridging Disaster Risk Management and Climate Adaptation
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe — EU Missions
opportunities.one_liner: This Horizon Europe call specifically funds projects bridging the gap between disaster risk management practices and climate adaptation planning across European regions and communities.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: HORIZON-MISS-2026-01-CLIMA-04 focuses on bridging the gap between disaster risk management and climate adaptation, with approximately €5 million available for a single project. The full suite of CLIMA Mission calls opened on 4 February 2026 and close on 23 September 2026. The EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change aims to support at least 150 European regions and communities towards climate resilience by 2030, helping regions and local authorities understand climate risks, develop adaptation pathways, and implement innovative solutions on the ground. This call is particularly relevant to teams working at the interface of early-warning systems, flood forecasting, and operational emergency management.
opportunities.eligibility: Research institutions, local authorities, businesses, NGOs in EU/Associated Countries (consortium required)

opportunities.title: Horizon Europe Cluster 3 (Civil Security) — Climate-Related Disaster Resilience Innovation Action
opportunities.funder: European Commission / Horizon Europe Cluster 3 (Disaster-Resilient Societies)
opportunities.one_liner: A Horizon Europe Cluster 3 Innovation Action building Europe's capacity to predict, prepare for, and respond to climate-related disasters including floods, using satellite data, AI, and cross-sector collaboration.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The European Commission is launching a Horizon Europe Innovation Action under Cluster 3 to strengthen Europe's resilience to climate-related disasters, with a deadline of November 5, 2026. This initiative addresses the increasing frequency and severity of climate-related disasters by improving Europe's ability to predict, prepare for, and respond to complex risks. By combining satellite data, AI, and cross-sector collaboration, it strengthens both immediate crisis response and long-term adaptation strategies. Eligible activities include early warning systems for floods, heatwaves, fires, and landslides, and the programme ensures that climate security solutions are inclusive and socially responsive.
opportunities.eligibility: Research consortia, civil protection agencies, local authorities, technology companies in EU/Associated Countries

opportunities.title: ADB — Ocean Resilience and Coastal Adaptation Trust Fund (ORCATF)
opportunities.funder: Asian Development Bank (ADB) — Ocean Resilience and Climate Adaptation Financing Partnership Facility
opportunities.one_liner: ADB's ORCATF provides grant co-financing specifically for nature-based solutions for coastal flood mitigation and protection in Asia-Pacific ADB member countries.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: A $1 million grant from the Ocean Resilience and Coastal Adaptation Trust Fund was recently deployed to finance nature-based solutions for flood mitigation and coastal protection in the Maldives. In 2024, ADB committed to climate finance reaching 50% of total annual committed financing by 2030, enabling over $100 billion in cumulative climate finance from its own resources across 2019 to 2030. ADB is promoting disaster risk reduction and climate change adaptation in the region by investing in flood control, transport, and energy infrastructure that withstands and builds resilience to disasters. Country governments and project developers in ADB developing member countries should engage ADB country operations departments to access ORCATF co-financing for eligible flood and coastal resilience projects.
opportunities.eligibility: ADB developing member country governments; accessed via ADB country operations departments
opportunities.budget: Grant tranches typically $1M–$3M per project component

opportunities.title: ADB — Asia-Pacific Climate Finance Fund (ACliFF)
opportunities.funder: Asian Development Bank (ADB) / Government of Germany (founding donor)
opportunities.one_liner: ACliFF is an ADB-managed multi-donor trust fund developing financial risk management products — including disaster insurance for city governments — to unlock climate resilience investment across Asia and the Pacific.
opportunities.summary_paragraph: The Asia-Pacific Climate Finance Fund is a multi-donor trust fund established in April 2017 with the objective of supporting the development and implementation of financial risk management products that can help unlock capital for climate investments and improve resilience to climate change impacts. The fund responds to the need to accelerate investment in climate change adaptation, addresses extreme weather events, and improves resilience to climate-related disasters — particularly for vulnerable and low-income populations — such as through disaster insurance for city governments to improve financial preparedness for disaster response. ACliFF is managed by ADB and was established with the Government of Germany as its first financing partner. Financial risk management products supported by the fund can be implemented alongside both ADB and third-party climate and development projects.
opportunities.eligibility: ADB member country governments and private sector entities via ADB nonsovereign operations
